commonIssues:
Many applicants face long delays or rejections when applying for new cards through MeeSeva centers, often due to strict eligibility checks or technical issues.

How to fix:

  • Ensure all required documents are correctly uploaded. Check your application status regularly on the EPDS Telangana portal.
  • If rejected without proper reason, visit the local Civil Supplies office or Mandal Revenue Office (MRO) to request a manual re-verification.

Failed biometric authentication at fair price shops prevents beneficiaries from receiving their monthly rations. eKYC issues are particularly common, leading to deactivation or suspension of cards.

How to fix:

  • Update your biometrics at the nearest Aadhaar center. Once updated, the e-POS machine will recognize your fingerprints.
  • For senior citizens or those with persistent fingerprint issues, request the dealer to use the OTP-based authentication (linked to Aadhaar mobile number).

Following Aadhaar linking, lakhs of ration cards have been cancelled, with many beneficiaries alleging their cards were deactivated without notice, accusing them of being "bogus" or having duplicates.

How to fix:

  • If your name or card is deleted, visit the MRO office with your Aadhaar and proof of residence to prove your eligibility and request restoration.
  • Provide a declaration stating that you do not hold any other ration card elsewhere.

Mismatches in names, Aadhaar details, or family member details listed in the system cause problems at the time of distribution.

How to fix:

  • Apply for data correction through the MeeSeva portal. Ensure that all names and dates of birth match the Aadhaar cards exactly.
  • Submit the correction request along with supporting documents to the local Civil Supplies Tahsildar for approval.

The government has been conducting intensive verification to remove in-eligible beneficiaries (such as those owning 4-wheelers or having high income), which has sometimes resulted in genuine cases being excluded.

How to fix:

  • If wrongly excluded, provide proof of your income status (Income Certificate) and a declaration that you do not own a 4-wheeler or any property exceeding the limit.
  • Submit these to the Civil Supplies authorities for re-verification of your eligibility.

The recent requirement to link ration cards with the "six guarantees" through Praja Palana has created pressure to update cards, causing difficulties for those without active cards.

How to fix:

  • Participate in the Praja Palana sabhas organized by the government to submit your ration card details for link-up with welfare schemes.
  • Ensure that your Aadhaar and mobile number are seeded to the ration card to enable seamless access to all government guarantee schemes.
